description: This figure presents a temperature-time schematic diagram illustrating the thermomechanical processing routes for Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels. The process begins with solution treatment at 1100-1200°C, followed by hot rolling with decreasing temperature. Subsequently, four distinct processing routes are depicted: Route 1 (green arrow) involves rapid cooling to room temperature after hot rolling followed by isothermal annealing; Route 2 (red arrow) incorporates cold rolling and recrystallization annealing at 1100-1200°C or intermediate temperatures to tailor δ-ferrite size, texture, and κ-carbide precipitation; Route 3 (blue arrow) and Route 4 (purple dashed arrow) represent aging treatments at 450-700°C. The caption states that cold rolling and annealing are employed to modify the coarse, lath-like δ-ferrite morphology present in hot-rolled steels, while aging at 500-700°C for 0.5-2 hours promotes nano-precipitate nucleation. These routes demonstrate systematic microstructure control through thermomechanical processing to achieve desired mechanical properties in austenite-base Fe-Mn-Al-C steels.
